classObjective: Identify and apply the reflexive, symmetric, and transitive properties of equality.
workedExamples: 1-3, Example 2 — Apply Properties of Equality to Complete Statements
1-3, Example 3 — Real-World Application: Toll Roads (Symmetric Property)
warmUp: Recall properties of equality from prior knowledge
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: reflexive (a=a), symmetric (if a=b then b=a), transitive (if a=b and b=c then a=c) with real-world toll road context
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying and applying properties of equality in statements and real-world contexts
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying properties of equality and applying them correctly
assessment: Exit ticket: identify the property used and apply it to complete a statement
capReflection: Why is the symmetric property particularly useful in real-world contexts like toll roads?
